DT-PHN-001.name: Phone Battery Not Charging Diagnostic Tree

steps.title: Test Cable and Charger
steps.instruction: Try a different certified cable and wall adapter. Attempt charging from a different outlet and also via a PC USB port. If the phone charges with a different cable, the cable is at fault.
steps.decision: Does the phone charge with a different cable/charger?

steps.title: Inspect Charging Port
steps.instruction: Shine a flashlight into the charging port. Look for lint, debris, bent pins, or corrosion. Use a wooden toothpick or soft brush to carefully remove any debris. Do not use metal tools inside the port.
steps.decision: Is debris or damage visible in the port?

steps.title: Soft Reset and Software Check
steps.instruction: Perform a soft reset (hold Power + Volume Down for 10 seconds). Boot into safe mode to rule out a rogue app consuming power. Check battery health in Settings > Battery.
steps.decision: Does the phone charge normally after soft reset?

steps.title: Battery Health Assessment
steps.instruction: Check battery health percentage (iOS: Settings > Battery > Battery Health; Android: use a battery diagnostic app). Below 80% capacity is considered degraded and replacement is recommended.

resolution_paths.action: Replace with MFi-certified cable and charger

resolution_paths.condition: Debris or lint in charging port

steps.title: Assess Display Functionality
steps.instruction: Check if the display still shows an image. Tap, swipe, and type across the entire screen to test touch responsiveness. Note any dark spots, lines, or areas with no touch response.
steps.decision: Is the display showing an image and responding to touch?

steps.title: Check for Digitizer Damage
steps.instruction: Test touch response in all four corners and the center. Try drawing or writing in a notes app. Dead zones or erratic touch indicate digitizer failure, not just a glass crack.
steps.decision: Are there dead zones or erratic touch behavior?

steps.title: Evaluate Repair vs. Replace
steps.instruction: Compare cost of screen replacement (typically $120-$350 for flagship phones) against device trade-in or replacement cost. If device is over 3 years old, factor in battery and overall condition.
steps.decision: Is screen repair cost less than 50% of device replacement cost?

resolution_paths.condition: Glass cracked but display and touch intact
resolution_paths.action: Apply tempered glass screen protector as temp fix; schedule glass-only replacement

resolution_paths.condition: Display dead or touch digitizer failed

DT-LAP-001.name: Laptop Won't Turn On Diagnostic Tree

steps.title: Check Power Supply
steps.instruction: Verify the charger LED is lit and charger is firmly connected. Try a different outlet. Inspect the charging port and cable for damage. Remove battery (if removable) and run on AC power only.
steps.decision: Does the laptop show any sign of life with confirmed-good charger?

steps.title: Perform Hard Reset
steps.instruction: Disconnect the charger. Hold the power button for 30 seconds to drain residual power. Reconnect charger and attempt power-on. This clears stuck power states common after updates or crashes.
steps.decision: Does the laptop power on after hard reset?

steps.title: Check RAM Seating
steps.instruction: Open the bottom access panel. Remove and firmly re-seat each RAM stick. Try booting with one stick at a time in the primary slot. A beep code on power-on indicates RAM is being detected.
steps.decision: Does the laptop POST (show manufacturer logo) with RAM re-seated?

steps.title: External Display Test
steps.instruction: Connect the laptop to an external monitor via HDMI or DisplayPort. Power on and check if the external display shows activity. This isolates whether the issue is the internal display or the motherboard/GPU.
steps.decision: Does the external display show output?

decision_points.question: No power with known-good charger?
decision_points.yes: Likely dead battery or DC jack — service needed

resolution_paths.action: Clean fan and replace thermal paste

resolution_paths.condition: Internal display failure with external output OK
resolution_paths.action: Replace display panel or display cable

DT-CON-001.name: Console HDMI Port Failure Diagnostic Tree

steps.title: Isolate Cable and TV
steps.instruction: Test the same HDMI cable on another device connected to the same TV port. Then try a different HDMI cable on the console. Test the console on a different TV or monitor.
steps.decision: Does the console output video on a different TV/cable combination?

steps.title: Inspect HDMI Port
steps.instruction: Shine a flashlight into the console's HDMI port. Look for bent or pushed-in pins, debris, or visible damage. A single bent pin is often repairable with fine tweezers.
steps.decision: Are pins bent or is there visible port damage?

steps.title: Safe Mode / Software Check
steps.instruction: Boot the console in Safe Mode (hold power button until second beep). Select 'Change Resolution' or 'Reset Video Output'. This can resolve black-screen issues from incorrect resolution settings.
steps.decision: Did Safe Mode video reset restore output?

decision_points.question: Works on different TV/cable?
decision_points.yes: Replace HDMI cable or fix TV HDMI port

decision_points.question: Safe Mode reset restored output?
decision_points.no: HDMI IC chip failure — professional service
